# Hypertube — built on Grobase

Hypertube is the 42 **"Hypertube"** subject: a BitTorrent **video search + streaming** web app —
register/log-in (Omniauth-style, ≥2 strategies), browse a paginated thumbnail library sourced from
≥2 legal torrent providers, open a video page (player, cast, year, rating, comments), and **stream a
torrent progressively** (playback starts as soon as enough is downloaded — never a full download
first), with on-the-fly transcode (mkv→playable), English/preferred-language subtitles, a month-idle
eviction sweep, and a **RESTful OAuth2 API**. *(Original subject: [`en.subject.txt`](en.subject.txt).)*

**There is no bespoke app server holding the data.** Hypertube runs on
**[Grobase](../../README.md)** — the self-hostable, Supabase-wire-compatible BaaS in this repo —
with auth, data, realtime, and storage all served by Grobase planes through one Kong gateway. Only
the three torrent/stream/search concerns that *cannot* be a generic CRUD call are small custom Go
services mounted behind the same gateway.

```
vendor/hypertube/
├── View/                 # the frontend: a React + Vite + TS SPA (auth, i18n, library, video, profile)
│   ├── public/
│   │   └── baas-config.js     # ← generated by the seed; window.__BAAS__ (url, anonKey, apiKey, mount ids, rt token)
│   ├── .env                   # ← generated by the seed; VITE_BAAS_* (DO NOT COMMIT)
│   └── src/                   # the app modules
├── grobase/              # the custom services (everything that is NOT generic CRUD)
│   ├── hypertube-media/       # torrent acquire + progressive Range stream + transcode + subtitles + eviction
│   ├── hypertube-search/      # ≥2 torrent sources + TMDb enrichment → the thumbnail library
│   └── hypertube-api/         # the subject's RESTful OAuth2 contract (POST /oauth/token, /users, /comments, /movies)
└── .env                  # provider secrets (TMDB_API_KEY, OPENSUBTITLES_API_KEY) — local, not committed
```

## What maps to what

| Concern | Hypertube subject | Grobase (now) |
|---|---|---|
| Auth (register, log-in, ≥2 Omniauth strategies) | Omniauth (42 + one more) | GoTrue `/auth/v1` — email/password **+** OAuth providers (42, Google); JWT identity |
| User profiles (username, picture, preferred lang) | app DB | Mongo `profiles` collection (**shared_resources** — any user may view another's public profile; email lives ONLY in GoTrue) |
| Catalog / library (thumbnails, popularity, year, rating) | scraped from torrent sources | Mongo `movies` collection (**shared_resources** — the library grid is world-readable), filled by `hypertube-search` |
| Comments on a video | app DB | Mongo `comments` collection (**shared_resources** — the prior-comment list is cross-owner readable) |
| Subtitles | downloaded per movie | Mongo `subtitles` collection (**shared_resources**) |
| Per-user watch state (watched / progress) | app DB | DynamoDB `watch_state` (keys `owner_pk`=user, `id`=movie; **owner-partitioned** — strictly per-user) |
| Server-side download/transcode jobs | background workers | DynamoDB `media_jobs` (keys `owner_pk`=`media:<movieId>`, `id`=jobId; attrs `state`, `pct`, `file_path`, `last_seen_at`) |
| Live comment updates | (polling) | Grobase realtime `/realtime/v1/ws` — `SUBSCRIBE table:<mongoDbId>:comments` |
| Torrent acquire + **progressive Range streaming** + transcode + subtitle fetch + eviction | bespoke streamer | **`hypertube-media`** (Go) behind Kong `/media/v1` |
| Search across ≥2 legal sources + TMDb enrichment | bespoke scraper | **`hypertube-search`** (Go) behind Kong `/search/v1` |
| RESTful OAuth2 API | bespoke controllers | **`hypertube-api`** (Go) behind Kong `/api` |

Everything the browser touches goes **only to Kong** (`/auth/v1`, `/query/v1/<mountId>`,
`/realtime/v1/ws`, `/storage/v1`, `/media/v1`, `/search/v1`, `/api`). No bespoke app database, no
ORM.

### Why a Mongo mount **and** a DynamoDB mount

The library/comments/profiles/subtitles are document-shaped and **world-readable** (the grid, the
prior-comment list, another user's public profile), so they live in **MongoDB** with
`shared_resources` set — cross-owner readable by the subject's contract. The watch-state and the
download/transcode jobs are **strictly per-user / per-movie key-value** with hot point reads, so they
live in **DynamoDB**, where the partition key *is* the owner — Grobase owner-scopes every request, so
a user can never read another user's `watch_state` row. Two engines, one tenant, one app key.

## The owner model (per request, never by pool state)

The seed registers the Mongo mount with
`shared_resources=["movies","profiles","comments","subtitles"]`, so those four collections are
readable across owners (the catalog grid, public profiles, the comment thread). Writes are still
owner-stamped (`owner_id: user:<sub>`). The DynamoDB mount carries **no** shared_resources — its rows
are owner-partitioned, so `watch_state` and `media_jobs` are private to the user/movie that owns
them. This is the same per-request RLS/owner-scoping the rest of the platform uses (it is what lets
`SHARE_POOLS` collapse many tenants onto one pool).

## Run it locally

```bash
# from the grobase repo root
make up                                                       # bring up the BaaS (Kong, GoTrue, Mongo, realtime, storage…)
docker compose --profile hypertube up -d --build              # the dynamodb-local + hypertube-{media,search,api} services
bash scripts/seed/hypertube-tenant.sh                         # idempotent: tenant + mbk_ key + Mongo mount + Dynamo mount
                                                              #   + collections/validators/indexes + GoTrue demo users
                                                              #   + writes View/public/baas-config.js, View/.env, .hypertube-baas.env
# the frontend (React + Vite + TS)
cd vendor/hypertube/View && npm ci && npm run build           # or: npm run dev  (Vite dev server)
```

- The seed discovers the **live Kong port** at runtime (`docker port mini-baas-kong 8000/tcp` —
  resolve-ports bumps busy ones) and writes it into `baas-config.js` / `.env` / `.hypertube-baas.env`.
- **DynamoDB is gated:** the Dynamo mount registers only when the engine is enabled (data plane built
  `--features dynamodb`, `dynamodb` in `isAllowedEngine`, and a `dynamodb-local` service in the
  hypertube profile). Until then the seed WARNs and finishes the Mongo half; `HT_DYNAMO_DB_ID` stays
  empty and the Dynamo gate SKIPs cleanly.

### Required `.env` keys (provider secrets — never commit, never hardcode)

Read **only** from env, default-warn (don't crash) when an optional key is empty:

| Key | Used by | What for |
|---|---|---|
| `TMDB_API_KEY` | `hypertube-search` | TMDb enrichment (rating, year, cover, cast) for the thumbnails + video page |
| `OPENSUBTITLES_API_KEY` | `hypertube-media` | English / preferred-language subtitle download |
| `FORTYTWO_CLIENT_ID` / `FORTYTWO_CLIENT_SECRET` | GoTrue (Omniauth strategy #1) | the **42** OAuth login |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` / `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` | GoTrue (Omniauth strategy #2) | the **Google** OAuth login |

`vendor/hypertube/.env` holds `TMDB_API_KEY` + `OPENSUBTITLES_API_KEY`; the OAuth provider secrets go
into the stack `.env` consumed by GoTrue. An empty optional key degrades gracefully (e.g. no TMDb key
→ thumbnails load without enrichment), exactly like the MovieVerse tmdb-proxy.

## Test it

Four self-contained verify gates exercise the round-trip **live** through Kong against the running
stack. Each SKIPs cleanly (exit 0) when its plane/service isn't up, so CI stays green; each has a
**load-bearing reject arm** (a vacuous gate is rejected). Bring the stack + hypertube profile up and
run the seed first.

```bash
bash scripts/verify/m150-hypertube-roundtrip.sh   # auth + Mongo CRUD + shared_resources cross-owner read + realtime EVENT
bash scripts/verify/m151-hypertube-dynamo.sh      # DynamoDB watch_state round-trip + partition isolation + media_jobs idempotent upsert
bash scripts/verify/m152-hypertube-rest.sh        # the RESTful OAuth2 contract (200/401/403/404) over Kong /api
bash scripts/verify/m153-hypertube-media.sh       # /media/v1/health + a real public-domain Range stream → 206 + Content-Range + X-Accel-Buffering:no
```

| Gate | The load-bearing proof |
|---|---|
| **m150** | GoTrue signup→JWT; Mongo `movies` insert/list/delete (201); user A inserts a comment, a **different** principal (user B) reads it back (proves `shared_resources` on Mongo); a realtime `SUBSCRIBE table:<mongoDbId>:comments` receives the change EVENT delivered to a non-writer. |
| **m151** | DynamoDB `watch_state` upsert/get round-trip; **partition isolation** — user B cannot read user A's `watch_state` row keyed on the same `id`; `media_jobs` idempotent upsert collapses to one row with the updated `state`. |
| **m152** | `POST /oauth/token` (client_credentials) → 200 + token, bad secret → 401; unauthenticated protected route → 401; `GET /users/{other}` hides the email; `PATCH /users/{other}` → 403; comment PATCH/DELETE by a non-owner → 403; unknown route → 404. |
| **m153** | `GET /media/v1/health` → 200; a real small public-domain torrent → `GET /stream` with `Range: bytes=0-1048575` asserts **206 Partial Content** + `Content-Range` + `X-Accel-Buffering: no` (progressive playback; archive.org is external so the stream arm is best-effort and SKIPs if no peers seed in the window). |

Beyond the gates:

- **Postman** — a collection exercising the RESTful API contract (`POST /oauth/token`, `GET /users`,
  `GET /users/:id`, `PATCH /users/:id`, `GET /movies`, `GET /movies/:id`, `GET /comments`,
  `GET /comments/:id`, `PATCH/DELETE /comments/:id`) with the subject's expected status codes.
- **Playwright** — an end-to-end browser run against `View/`: register/login (email + an OAuth
  strategy), search → paginated thumbnails (infinite scroll), open a video → player starts streaming
  progressively, post a comment and see it appear live (realtime), edit own profile, attempt to edit
  another user's profile (rejected).
